zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeremiah York於1683年9月9日出生在英國巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼時，他的父親Richard York II32歲，母親Ann Seymour31歲。他於1707年與Sarah Seymour在英國巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1729年住在美國馬里蘭州巴爾的摩卡羅爾且於1733年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州弗雷德里克。他去世於1765年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡羅來納州奧拉享年82歲，埋葬在美國北卡羅來納州蘭多夫利波迪桑迪 可可 巴普蒂斯特 邱奇 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeremiah York于1683年9月9日出生在英国巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼时，他的父亲Richard York II32岁，母亲Ann Seymour31岁。他于1707年与Sarah Seymour在英国巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1729年住在美国马里兰州巴爾的摩卡羅爾且于1733年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州弗雷德里克。他去世于1765年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡罗来纳州奧拉享年82岁，埋葬在美国北卡罗来纳州蘭多夫利波迪桑迪 可可 巴普蒂斯特 邱奇 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: York
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

variants.name: Yurkovich
meanings: Ukrainian (standard transliteration Yurkovych) and Belorussian: patronymic from the personal name Yurko and its Belorussian equivalent Yurka, pet forms of Ukrainian Yuriy and Belorussian Yuray, equivalents of Greek Geōrgios (see George ).
Americanized form of Croatian Jurković, Slovak and Slovenian Jurkovič (see Jurkovic , compare Jurkovich and Yurkovic ).
Jewish (from Bessarabia): habitational name for someone from Yurkovtsy in Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: Jeremiah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Meaning ‘appointed by God’ in Hebrew; it was borne in the Bible by a Hebrew prophet of the 7th–6th centuries  bc , whose story, prophecies of judgement, and lamentations are recorded in the book of the Bible that bears his name. The Book of Lamentations is also attributed to him; it bewails the destruction of Jerusalem and the temple by the Babylonians in  587   bc . Despite (or because of) the gloomy subject matter of these texts, the name enjoyed some popularity among Puritans from the 16th century onwards and later Christian fundamentalists, partly perhaps because Jeremiah also preached reconciliation with God after his wrath was assuaged. Since the 1970s it has found considerable favour in the United States.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: The Tuscarora War
historicEvents.description: The British, Dutch, and German settlers fought with the Tuscarora Native Americans, from September 22, 1711- February 11, 1715, in North Carolina.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

historicEvents.title: Becomes a Royal Colony
historicEvents.description: July 25, 1729, North Carolina became a royal colony, when the colony was sold to King George II.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Anchor-Carolina Becomes North and South Carolina

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1729: Baltimore, Maryland Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Baltimore, Maryland, United States 1851: *Baltimore City, Maryland, United States.  *Became Independent City from Baltimore County.

historicEvents.title: Gregorian Calendar is Adopted
historicEvents.description: Gregorian calendar was adopted in England in 1752. That year, Wednesday, September 2, 1752, was followed by Thursday, September 14th, 1752, which caused the country to skip ahead eleven days.

historicEvents.title: Seven Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Seven Years' War began as a North American conflict then stretched between England and France. England, along with allies, battled France in America, India, and Europe, making it arguably the first global war. The conflict ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763 and England was victorious. The Seven Years' war ultimately led to discontent in the colonies and the American Revolution.

en.lifeSummary: When Jeremiah York was born on 9 September 1683, in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Richard York II, was 32 and his mother, Ann Seymour, was 31. He married Sarah Seymour in 1707, in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He lived in Carroll, Baltimore, Maryland, United States in 1729 and Frederick, Virginia, British Colonial America in 1733. He died in 1765, in Orange, North Carolina, British Colonial America, at the age of 82, and was buried in Sandy Creek Baptist Church Cemetery, Liberty, Randolph, North Carolina, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Jeremiah York(이)가 1683년 9월 9일 영국 벅잉함실르 니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard York II(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Ann Seymour(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1707년 영국 벅잉함실르 니에서 Sarah Seymour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1729년도에 미국 메릴랜드 주 바팀어 카로에서, 1733년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프레더릭크에서 살았다. 그는 1765년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 오랑에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프 리버티 샌디 크리크 바프티스트 c후르치 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeremiah York nasceu em 9 de setembro de 1683, em Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard York II, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Ann Seymour, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Seymour em 1707, em Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele viveu em Carroll, Baltimore, Marilândia, Estados Unidos da América em 1729 e em Frederick, Virginia, British Colonial America em 1733. Ele faleceu em 1765, em Orange, Carolina do Norte, British Colonial America, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em Sandy Creek Baptist Church Cemetery, Liberty, Randolph, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeremiah York родился 9 сентября 1683 г. в местности олни, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard York II было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Ann Seymour, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Seymour в 1707 г. в местности олни, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он жил в местности карролл, балтимор, Мэриленд, Соединенные штаты в 1729 году и в местности фредирикк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1733 году. Он умер в 1765 г. в местности оранг, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности сэнди крик баптист чурч кеметири, либирти, рандолф, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты.
